title: Sleep-Related Thought Record
description: Use this worksheet to identify and challenge unhelpful thoughts about sleep that may be perpetuating insomnia or sleep anxiety.
instructions: When you notice anxious or negative thoughts about sleep — whether at bedtime, during the night, or during the day — complete each section below to examine and reframe those thoughts.

fields.label: 1. Situation — When did this thought occur? (e.g., lying in bed, waking at 3 AM, thinking about tonight's sleep)
fields.placeholder: Describe the situation that triggered your sleep-related thought...

fields.label: 2. Automatic Thought — What went through your mind about sleep?
fields.placeholder: e.g., 'I'll never fall asleep tonight', 'Tomorrow will be ruined if I don't sleep well'

fields.label: 3. Anxiety Level — How anxious or distressed did this thought make you? (0 = none, 100 = extreme)
4. Thinking Trap — Which unhelpful pattern best fits this thought?

options.label: Fortune Telling ('I know I won't sleep tonight')
All-or-Nothing ('If I don't get 8 hours, the night is wasted')
Emotional Reasoning ('I feel wired, so I won't be able to sleep')
Should Statements ('I should be able to sleep like a normal person')

fields.label: 5. Evidence For — What supports this thought?
fields.placeholder: What evidence seems to support this sleep-related worry?

fields.label: 6. Evidence Against — What contradicts this thought?
fields.placeholder: Have you managed okay after poor sleep before? Is the predicted outcome guaranteed?

fields.label: 7. Balanced Thought — What is a more realistic perspective on this sleep situation?
fields.placeholder: e.g., 'One bad night won't ruin me. My body knows how to recover. I've coped before.'

fields.label: 8. Re-rate Anxiety — How anxious do you feel now? (0 = none, 100 = extreme)

distortionsList.name: Catastrophizing
distortionsList.description: Predicting the worst possible outcome from poor sleep, such as 'My entire week will be ruined.'

distortionsList.name: Fortune Telling
distortionsList.description: Predicting you won't sleep before even trying, based on past experiences rather than current evidence.

distortionsList.name: All-or-Nothing Thinking
distortionsList.description: Viewing sleep in black-and-white terms: either a 'perfect night' or a 'total failure.'

distortionsList.name: Overgeneralization
distortionsList.description: Using words like 'always' or 'never' about sleep patterns based on a few bad nights.

distortionsList.name: Emotional Reasoning
distortionsList.description: Assuming that feeling alert or anxious at bedtime means you definitely won't sleep.

distortionsList.name: Should Statements
distortionsList.description: Rigid rules about sleep such as 'I should fall asleep within 10 minutes' or 'I should need exactly 8 hours.'

disclaimer: This worksheet is for educational purposes only and is not a substitute for professional mental health treatment. If you have persistent sleep problems, consult a healthcare provider or seek CBT-I treatment.
